items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (attempt 3 — source verified, line numbers confirmed). ✅ DEPENDENCY CLEARED: BUG-084 shipped (sprint-1774323650, 2026-03-23). BUG-085 also shipped (sprint-1774323983). Belt-and-suspenders fix is now the full intent — no blockers remain. EXACT CHANGES REQUIRED (2 source files + 2 test files): FILE 1: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts — Line 13 (verified): change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. Line 53 (verified): change 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })' to 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })'. FILE 2: src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts — After line 13 (last existing import): add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";'. Line 51 (verified): change 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })' to 'mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })'. BEFORE WRITING TESTS: Grep credentials.ts and snapshot-registry.ts for 'export' to confirm public API. If setProviderCredential / cloudCredentialsPath / addSnapshot are not exported, identify the correct exported function names before writing the test imports. FILE 3: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.test.ts — mkdtempSync tmpdir in beforeEach, rmSync in afterEach. Test 1: call setProviderCredential(testDir, 'digitalocean', 'test-token'), statSync(join(testDir, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. Test 2: statSync(cloudCredentialsPath(testDir)).mode & 0o777 === 0o600. FILE 4: src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.test.ts — call addSnapshot(testDir, snapshotRecord), statSync(join(testDir, 'cloud')).mode & 0o777 === 0o700. SnapshotRecord fields: snapshotId, provider, region, builderSize, clawhqVersion, builtAt (ISO string), name. AFTER CHANGES: run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass. DO NOT add chmodSync to snapshot-registry — snapshots.json is non-sensitive, only the directory needs 0700. Previous fail (attempt 3) was implementation failure, not spec failure — steps are correct.

items.title: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json written without mode 0600 — security config and health state world-readable
items.intent: trust-mode.json and heartbeat.json must be created with mode 0600 — trust-mode.json reveals the agent security posture and heartbeat.json reveals agent activity patterns. Both must be protected the same way credentials.json is.
items.description: src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts line 75: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. trust-mode.json stores the agent trust mode (Paranoid/Zero-Trust/Managed) — revealing the security posture on a multi-user system allows an attacker to know whether the agent is configured to auto-approve cloud operations. src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts line 71: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content) has no mode option. heartbeat.json stores health status and timing data. Both files follow the same write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern as commands.json (BUG-086) but without FILE_MODE_SECRET protection. Fix pattern is identical to BUG-086: add chmodSync import, add FILE_MODE_SECRET to existing defaults.js import, change writeFileSync to include { mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }, and add chmodSync before renameSync. switch.ts has no existing defaults.js import — add one alongside the DIR_MODE_SECRET import added by BUG-085. reporter.ts already imports CLOUD_HEARTBEAT_RPC_TIMEOUT_MS from defaults.js — add FILE_MODE_SECRET there.
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23 (Tech Lead). BUG-085 shipped (confirmed in recent_sprints — sprint-1774323983), so the DIR_MODE_SECRET import dependency is met in both switch.ts and reporter.ts. Steps verified against grooming notes: exact file paths (switch.ts line ~75, reporter.ts line ~71), exact import pattern (extend existing defaults.js import, add chmodSync to node:fs destructure), exact write pattern (mode option + chmodSync before renameSync). Steps include: grep for exported functions before writing tests — prior failure pattern on BUG-078 suggests assuming exported names without verifying causes test failures. Pattern identical to BUG-086 (commands.json, already shipped). No scope creep: only switch.ts and reporter.ts. DO NOT add chmodSync to directory creation — BUG-085 covers directories.
